opp.name: Mair Group S2P
opp.next_step: Confidence 100% 20th June - Year 1 licences invoice issued. 18th June - Sales order completed, PO shared with finance. 17th June - Full initialled/stamped/signed copy received. 12th June - 11th hour issues resolved, contract updated, signed by Carl and shared with CPO, he has confirmed he will be signing it today. SIGNED SIGNATURE PAGE RECEIVED. 11th June - CPO is trying to push through some additional negotiations at the 11th hour, most are OK, working with Carl to formulate a response. 10th June - The Legal-2-legal call happed today with the CPO Mazin in attendance, There was one issue they (Mazin + Legal) were VERY firm on regarding arbitration (They insisted on AD courts, which creates a language issue ... compared to a Dubai option which is in English) - after some strong push back Zoheb conceded (it is a Mair Group board resolution) to their demand, so all issues now resolved. Final sanity check undertaken by both sides, final clean copy shared and signed by Zycus (Carl) and awaiting their counter signature tomorrow. 9th June - Final markups received today, Zoheb reviewed quickly and reverted - some back & forth and a hard push from them on termination for convenience, with Zoheb & Carl's help we submitted and they accepted the standard clause, all outstanding issues appear resolved and a clean final version has been shared, but they want a call on Weds 10th afternoon to discuss, if all well on that call we should be able to push for immediate signature. 4th June - S ome meetings & positive activity today, most of the legal responses are ready to share with us, just 2 IT elements they need to resolve internally, then they will share the feedback (won't say what they are at this stage, but say nothing to worry about) - pushing for today but they won't commit, so likely tomorrow (Fri)..... it's clear now their legal are actively pushing back on a call ... maybe after this feedback we can try again if needed. Mazin (CPO) is back today (he was ill for a few days), I have asked him for his help progressing this - he is not promising but I know he is asking questions in the background which will help, he is sticking to his point that it's the use of our paper that's causing delays ( not really true , they have had the templates for quite some time, and we were open to using theirs if quicker).... I am still pushing for a sig tomorrow, but realistically looking unlikely as there will be another markup iteration to work through. 3rd June - P roc team are still pushing legal and are now expecting an update tomorrow (Thurs) - I keep pushing the legal2Legal call but they are not biting.. They tell me there are no issues, lets see when we get the feedback no one has mentioned T4C, and I am not reminding them. Mazin the CPO is off again today, so no response on the signing 'ceremony' (they think he is back tomorrow), I am still pushing for ASAP/Friday. 2nd June - P roc team are chasing legal, hoping to have it resolved Thurs latest, they are not aware of any issues/showstoppers - I reiterated the call option with Zoheb and they will push their own legal again to arrange (if needed).... The CPO/signatory is off today so no answer on the signing ceremony with Carl, I am pushing for Thurs or Fri, they understand the urgency of booking your travel and will update me tomorrow on his return- if they agree to sig Friday it's a good sign for the contract negotiations/T4C. 1st June - No update last week as the team were all off for the Eid public holiday, caught up this morning for an update: they have a meeting with Legal today to get an update on progress, they have agreed to a direct call with Zoheb and their legal (Omar) early this week to resolve any outstanding issues and move towards signature. 25th May - MSA & OF1 review underway, initial MG/Zycus markups shared on MSA, no counters/follow-ups from Mair yet, chased Mon 25th - pushing to get it resolved asap, now likely after Eid public holiday (for rest of the week). 22nd May - MSA and Order 1 close to completion with Mair legal- will have full update by Monday 25th May 19th - Redlines on MSA received today, (thanks to Zoheb for a quick turnaround) - some back and forth on clauses and a negotiation needed on a termination for convenience - reverted. May 18th - Following emergency below, Mair head of legal has agreed to continue reviewing our paper, feedback/redlines expected Tues 19th or Weds 20th (Zoheb continues to review theirs in parallel). May 14th - Head of legal working on our paper has had to leave the country for a family emergency, unclear on his availability to resolve currently - Zoheb is working on their template as a fallback and will update me on 19th May. May 13th - Lovemer has a meeting on 14th with Omar (Mair Head of Legal) to discuss the MSA/OF will update us asap afterwards with feedback. May 12th - Reminded them for the SOW workshop - Hrishi +1 (& Dan) will travel and hold onsite to minimise any risk, date TBC. May 11th - Mair legal are reviewing Zycus MSA and hopeful to agree on using our paper- will know in next 2 days (this will greatly accelerate signing) No immediate blockers in Ts & Cs. May 8th - LOI received - likely MSA/Order 1 sign off "before Eid break" (i.e. before 26th May) - pushing for sooner. May 7th - All issues resolved, some back & forth on implementation phasing, but nothing to worry about, verbal comment from Head of Proc Ops: "Everything appears to be ok for approval today/tomorrow & LOI early next week". May 6th - Meeting below held: (positive session), mostly based on clarifications of: a) implementation invoicing across phases (resolved), b) number of onsite teams (resolved), c) travel budget (Outstanding - they are asking us to swallow it all - we are pushing back, to split 50/50 as this stage - action for them to confirm, otherwise potentially intro Carl to Mazin-CPO), d) we also raised the legal paperwork, their policy/preference is their own standard template, (Outstanding - not a showstopper, but made a case that our paper is Saas/Zycus fit for purpose and faster/easier to edit than rewriting theirs, action for them to confirm). I ntention is to resolve these issues by the end of the week, ideally then to submit LOI by EOW (Fri 8th May) and signed Contracts before Eid public holiday (w/c Mon 25th May). May 4th - Provided some clarifications (Implementation onsite support, payment terms, travel) - they followed up with a request for a call on Weds 6th to discuss: Phased Implementation plan timeline, Payment Terms and Travel fees - Carl, Tanya & Hrishi are joining, (also meeting on 5th to strategize). 30th April/May 1st - Some back & forth clarifications, nothing concerning. 29th April - Comment from CPO after a nudge: "Still going through some internal review process. Hope to close this asap". 27th April - Requested (already sent) and resent MSA & Oder Form templates for internal review, also completed a vendor registration form and provided documentation (Trade Licence, TRN, Bank Details etc.), made clear that using our paper is preferred but not a showstopper. 24th April - They acknowledged receipt of BAFO, are reviewing will revert next week, looking like they will push for their paper, asked to discuss with CPO as ours is Saas/Zycus fit for purpose, faster, mutual, no risk etc. etc. 23rd April - Liaised with Carl offered some BAFO concessions (through discount options held over for this phase e.g. Publicity 5%), license pricing mostly protected in return for: a) min 5 years, b) strong reference-ability/PR and c) speed of decision (end May signature latest) - BAFO & MSA/Oder Form templates shared - Next Step: requested a call with CPO Fri/Sat/Sun to discuss. 21st April - F2F meeting went well, asking us to sweeten the deal in year 1, but in return have agreed to 5 years (started the meeting committing to only 3), will be a reference customer and a quick decision - followed up with an mail confirming & asking for closing BAFO price with preference to Zycus- some niggly terms we should be able to navigate, due Thursday, with formal decision expected by mid-May, liaising with Carl to review. 16th April - Next step is a face-to-face meeting in Abu Dhabi to discuss commercials & implementation, Dan & Tanya will be on-site with Hrishi dialling in - will synch with Carl prior to discuss commercial tactics. 9th April - Clarifications and revised implementation pricing (now a longer phased approach) hand delivered - retained original SaaS pricing in expectation of a negotiation (champion tip), made clear the assumption is a single phased contract, digital copies also added to their Showpad link. NOTE: Some issues where we were not able to meet their requirements/match GEP i.e. RTO/RPO (concerns due to current war situation and AWS taken out in Dubai & Bahrain) - champion thinks it won't be a showstopper. 6th April - Revised phasing and further formal clarifications received - response to be hard copy/hand delivered to Abu Dhabi office Thurs latest. 3rd April - Positive clarifications call, some push to improve RTO/RPO and uptime to match the competition, but doesn't appear to be a showstopper, also looking to move away from big bang to a phased approach focussing on priority modules e.g. Sourcing first, not clear economic impact yet, but have indicated it will still be a single contract - follow up call with the champion afterwards confirmed informally we are the preferred vendor and are in competition with GEP, next steps they are going to confirm on 6th/7th what their phasing plan will be and we will need to review/update commercials accordingly - note GEP are 50% of our pricing in the Al Tasnim opp. 1st April - Clarification responses run-through call scheduled for Fri 3rd April. 30th Mar - Clarifications received and responses submitted. 25th March - Nudged Lovemer for an update on evaluation & timelines, Vishal (CTO) called later to clarify some RFP response elements, potentially a good sign as he said he is not supposed to do it and it's against the proc policies to give us an advantage, will clarify and respond asap today/tomorrow. 6th March - RFP Technical & Commercial response hand delivered to HQ in Abu Dhabi, response clarifications is next step. (Commercial positioned as a budgetary estimate as we had to guesstimate many data points, also held back on some discounts in anticipation of further negotiation). 26th Feb - Clarifications received (still many gaps, will need to guesstimate and make caveats in pricing) - deadline extended to 6th March - still expecting a hand delivered hard copy to Abu Dhabi. 10th Feb - RFP for S2P received - deadline is 25th Feb, clarifications 18th.... currently requires hard copy submission to Abu Dhabi, requested Showpad or alternative. SEE ATTACHED NOTES FOR HISTORY REMOVED DUE TO LACK OF SPACE
opp.ais_why:
• Across 6,960 chars of dated rep notes spanning Nov 2024 to May 2026 ‚Äî covering disco call, demos, RFP submission, BAFO, MSA negotiation and LOI ‚Äî there is zero buyer-side or rep-side AI mention. Product scope is iContract; iSource; eProcurement (no Merlin, no Agentic AI, no ANA, no Merlin Intake). The 7 May 2026 entry quotes Head of Proc Ops: 'Everything appears to be ok for approval today/tomorrow & LOI early next week' ‚Äî pure commercial close, not AI.
• On 21 Apr 2026: 'F2F meeting went well, asking us to sweeten the deal in year 1, but in return have agreed to 5 years.' On 24 Apr 2026: 'They acknowledged receipt of BAFO, are reviewing will revert next week.' Discussions are about pricing, travel budget split, MSA paper, RTO/RPO concerns ‚Äî competitive with GEP at 50% of Zycus pricing. AI features never surface as differentiator or buyer ask.
• Confidence is High: 6 months of late-stage negotiation detail with no AI signal whatsoever and no AI SKU in scope. This is a traditional procurement automation deal evaluated on price, implementation phasing, and SaaS-vs-on-premise considerations.

items.quote: the subscription should commence once the product has been fully developed and actively put into use

items.area: Owner-transition continuity (Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim)
items.quote: I copy Mohamad Alhakim, the new Zycus VP, as courtesy and introduction to Mair team
items.source: Clari email 26 Jun 2026; field history 1 Jul 2026

metrics.narrative: The business problem is well established: Mair Group is a five-company merger trying to harmonize fragmented procurement across three ERPs (SAP S/4HANA, SAP B1 and Yardi), and Mazin noted on the 21 Apr 2026 call that his people were still 'submitting hard copies, which I said I have never done in over a decade and a half', framing the transformation mandate. Zycus positioned a value-based delivery model with signed-off KPIs (Hrishikesh cited reducing vendor-onboarding cycle time from 21 to 15 days as an illustrative target on the 21 Apr call). However the ROI/savings were never quantified by the buyer in dollar terms, so the value case remains qualitative. Quantifying the KPI baseline with Ranjit and Mazin during Phase 1 scoping is the move to firm this up.

champion.narrative: The deal closed on strong economic sponsorship rather than a classic coaching champion. Vishal Kapil (Group CIO & CTO, Decision Maker) is a credible technical validator with prior Zycus exposure and joined every major discovery/demo session, but he is a validator, not an internal seller. Mazin (Director of Procurement, economic buyer) drove the deal to signature but was making the decision, not coaching Zycus. Lovemer Raymundo is the operational coordinator and the most frequent Mair-side contact and will be the Phase 1 project lead. What is developed: clear sponsorship and a signed contract; what is missing: an internal advocate who navigates escalation and defends Zycus if Phase 1 stumbles. Building Lovemer into that role with early Phase 1 wins is the forward action.

competition.narrative: Competition is effectively over: GEP Worldwide was the single live peer threat (priced ~50% below Zycus per the 3 Apr Next Step note), but Mair confirmed Zycus as preferred vendor on 3 Apr, issued the LOI 8 May and signed 12-17 Jun, so GEP is out. Coupa (previously implemented and abandoned as overcomplicated) and SAP Ariba (licences written off) are historical cautionary context, and Ivalua/JAGGAER/Oracle are awareness-set names only. Zycus won on speed-to-value, pre-built SAP/Yardi connectors, unified three-ERP experience, regional presence and SaaS-fit paper. The only residual competitive risk is a GEP re-approach if Phase 1 delivery falters, which is why protecting the go-live matters.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is concrete and buyer-articulated: Mair is 'born out of 5 different company merger' with people carrying different ways of working, and Mazin is 'trying hard to harmonize into one way of doing things' (Vishal, 21 Apr call). Procurement was still running on manual hard-copy submissions to vendors, which Mazin flagged as embarrassingly outdated. The technical pain owner is Vishal Kapil (Group CIO & CTO), who owns the multi-ERP integration risk (SAP + Yardi, with Yardi flagged as an inflexible third party that could delay the project); the process/transformation pain owner is Mazin. This is confirmed operational and organizational pain, not inferred.

paper_process.narrative: Zycus won the paper battle: the MSA and Order Form 1 were executed on Zycus's SaaS-fit template rather than Mair's standard template, after Dan argued repeatedly (6 May call) that rewriting Mair's paper for SaaS would waste weeks. The final blocker was legal jurisdiction: on the 10 Jun legal-to-legal call, Mair's Head of Legal (Omar) and CPO insisted on exclusive Abu Dhabi courts as a non-negotiable board resolution, and Zycus VP Legal Zoheb conceded and deleted the arbitration clause to close it. Signed via DocuSign, Carl Kimball as Zycus authorized signatory and Mazin for Mair. Remaining paper: the SOW (must be signed to trigger delivery kickoff) and an NDA Lovemer sent for Zycus to complete and return on 29 Jun.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Mazin Abdulhadi Hawasli, Director of Procurement)
economic_buyer.narrative: Mazin Abdulhadi Hawasli (Director of Procurement) is the economic buyer and commercial authority: he opened the 21 Apr F2F by promising to pull out 'a big baseball bat and asking you to give me a nice little reduction in price. About 49%', drove the entire pricing negotiation, and countersigned the agreement on Mair's behalf on 12 Jun (per DocuSign process agreed on the 10 Jun legal call). Above him sits an SVP who signed off the commercials, referenced by Dan on the 6 May call ('get Mazin to talk to the senior vice president who signed all this off'), the ultimate budget owner. Mazin is fully engaged and bought-in (last contact 29 Jun email). His authority showed again in the invoice dispute where his team, not Zycus, set the payment-timing terms.

decision_process.narrative: The process is fully visible from the call and Next Step sequence: RFP received 10 Feb, technical + commercial response hand-delivered to the relevant stakeholder 6 Mar, clarifications through March, F2F Abu Dhabi 21 Apr, BAFO late April, LOI 8 May, MSA/Order Form redlines through May, legal-to-legal call 10 Jun, signature 12-17 Jun, PO/invoice 18-20 Jun, SOW workshop 29 Jun, and formal Phase 1 kickoff next. The approval chain runs procurement operations (Lovemer Raymundo, Maria Fe Briones) up to Mazin (Director of Procurement) up to the SVP sign-off, with a Mair board resolution governing legal terms (jurisdiction). Ranjit Warang is driving the SOW/kickoff scheduling and owns the SAP integration.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit from the RFP and negotiation: pre-built integration to their three ERPs (SAP S/4HANA, SAP B1, Yardi) with minimal custom build, a phased rollout (Phase 1 sourcing + PR-to-PO, then supplier management, then contract/invoice), SaaS-fit contracting on a fast paper trail, competitive price benchmarked against GEP (noted at 50% of Zycus pricing on the 3 Apr Next Step), regional Middle East presence, and commercial concessions (5-year term, phased payment milestones, travel split). Vishal validated the technical fit and repeatedly stress-tested the integration approach on the 21 Apr call. Zycus won on speed-to-value and pre-built connectors rather than lowest price.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're well-positioned — the real risk is timing and sign-off, not a competitor.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Contract In Progress' anchors near 85. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 99.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — The deal closed on strong economic sponsorship rather than a classic coaching champion. Vishal Kapil (Group CIO & CTO, Decision Maker) is a credible technical validator who joined every major session but is a validator, not an internal seller; Mazin (Director of Procurement) drove the deal to signature as the economic buyer, not as a coach. Lovemer Raymundo, the most frequent Mair contact and likely Phase 1 project lead, is the best candidate to build into an active champion by giving him early Phase 1 wins. At this stage (signed, executing) the absence of a coaching champion is a Phase-1 execution watch-item, not a deal risk.
bullets.text: Champion is driving it — The deal closed on strong economic sponsorship rather than a classic coaching champion.

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: No active competitor as of 3 Jul 2026: the deal is signed and executing.

bullets.text: Mair is resisting the upfront licence invoice, insisting billing start at go-live; 60-day payment terms proposed as compromise but not yet accepted.
Account ownership changed from the relevant stakeholder to Mohamad Alhakim mid-transaction; relationship continuity with Mazin, Vishal and Ranjit must be preserved.
Yardi (third-party US real-estate ERP) flagged by Vishal as inflexible and a likely source of Phase 1 integration delay.

blocks.title: Customer commitment

Lock the SOW workshop output into a signed SOW to trigger delivery kickoff. (by 10 Jul)

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Mair Group — Contract In Progress · $266K · closes 28 May

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 99 is carried by preference strength +1.00 (weight 20); exec access strength +1.00 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 66 (moving forward): commercial topics entering (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 25 reflects real customer investment: Direct economic-buyer access recorded.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 96 is anchored on the stage win (99), nudged by commitment 25 and momentum 66, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.
Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 2 / Negative and the calls corroborate it: zero AI or Merlin discussion across discovery, demos, RFP and negotiation, and no AI SKU in scope. The rating is accurate, not under-rated; AI is genuinely not part of this buyer's evaluation.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (five-company merger, fragmented three-ERP procurement, manual hard-copy processes) and a KPI-driven value-based delivery model, but no buyer-quantified ROI or savings figure.

critical_signals.text: The SOW workshop with the full Mair and Zycus teams took place 29 June and the next step is the formal Phase 1 kickoff, so delivery mobilisation has begun.
Mair is refusing to pay the licence invoice before go-live and Carl negotiated 60-day terms on 26 June, a friction still awaiting the buyer's confirmation.
Mohamad Alhakim, the new Zycus regional VP, took over the account from Dan Quinn on 1 July and has been introduced to Mazin's team, so continuity must be actively managed.
GEP was the only live rival at roughly half of Zycus pricing and is out since Mair selected and signed with Zycus, leaving no active competitor into delivery.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and healthy post-signature: incoming buyer emails 29 Jun, a meeting 3 Jul, contract signed and PO received. Momentum is on delivery mobilisation, with the invoicing dispute the only friction to watch.

north_star_verdict.math: Today 3 Jul 2026. Close date 28 May 2026 passed 36 days ago; contract actually signed 12-17 Jun (real milestone), so the placeholder close slipped ~20 days. Deal age 228 days (created 17 Nov 2025). ~21 days in Contract In Progress since 12 Jun signature. End-to-end cycle 12 Nov 2025 discovery to 17 Jun 2026 execution (~7 months) is reasonable for a $265k first-sale enterprise deal with RFP, three-ERP scoping and legal redlines.
evidence: Contract fully executed: signed signature page received 12 Jun 2026, full initialled/stamped/signed copy received 17 Jun 2026 (source: Next Step; email 'Signed Agreement - PO4800686635' from glenice.keen@mairgroup.com 17 Jun).
PO 4800686635 received and licence invoice (No. 141/DXB-2026-27) issued 18-20 Jun 2026 (source: Next Step; task emails 18-23 Jun).
SOW workshop held 29 Jun 2026 with full Mair + Zycus teams (source: event 'SOW Workshop' 29 Jun; email thread 18-25 Jun).

Invoice-timing dispute opened 23-26 Jun: Mair insists 'the subscription should commence once the product has been fully developed and actively put into use'; Carl secured 60-day payment terms as compromise (source: Clari emails 23-26 Jun 2026).

Legal jurisdiction resolved 10 Jun 2026: Mair board resolution required exclusive Abu Dhabi courts (non-negotiable); Zycus VP Legal Zoheb conceded and deleted the arbitration clause to finalize (source: 2026-06-10 legal call, Omar/Zoheb/Mazin).
Account ownership changed Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim (Regional VP) on 1 Jul 2026; Carl introduced Mohamad to the Mair team 26 Jun (source: field history 2026-07-01; email 26 Jun).
Buyer engagement is live: incoming emails 29 Jun and a 'Mair Group/ Zycus project' meeting 3 Jul 2026, Last Activity Date 3 Jul (source: activities).
north_star_verdict.headline: Contract is fully executed (signed 12 Jun, full copy 17 Jun), PO received 18 Jun, licence invoice issued 20 Jun and the SOW workshop was held 29 Jun, so the sale is essentially won. Two fresh frictions keep this at Close Date Risk not clean On Track: Mair is disputing the upfront licence invoice (wants billing to start at go-live; Carl negotiated 60-day terms 26 Jun, awaiting confirmation), and account ownership moved from Dan Quinn to Mohamad Alhakim on 1 Jul mid-transaction. The close date (28 May) is long past; the real milestone was signature (mid-June, done). Remaining path is the invoicing agreement plus formal Phase 1 kickoff.

flags: Get the phased-invoicing / 60-day compromise confirmed in writing by Mazin before Phase 1 kickoff to protect cash and goodwill.
Manage the Dan-to-Mohamad ownership handoff deliberately so the Mazin, Vishal and Ranjit relationships transfer without a cadence gap.
De-risk the Yardi (third-party ERP) integration early in Phase 1 scoping, the one dependency Vishal flagged as a project-delay risk.
best_practice_check.summary: Late-stage, signed deal: the win-strategy levers now protect the executed contract, the cash and the go-live rather than the sale itself.

customer_preference.evidence: Mair confirmed Zycus as preferred vendor (3 Apr Next Step), issued the LOI and signed the contract 12-17 Jun, agreeing a 5-year term and to be a reference customer.

competitive_position.summary: No active competitor as of 3 Jul 2026: the deal is signed and executing. GEP Worldwide was the only live peer (50% below Zycus pricing per 3 Apr 2026 note) and is OUT since Zycus was selected 3 Apr and signed 17 Jun. Coupa (abandoned as overcomplicated) and SAP Ariba (licences written off) are historical; Ivalua, JAGGAER and Oracle are awareness-set names never raised on calls; Kissflow was a build-vs-buy alternative resolved in Zycus's favour. The only residual threat is a GEP re-approach if Phase 1 delivery stumbles.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Follow up call with the champion afterwards confirmed informally we are the preferred vendor and are in competition with GEP. Note GEP are 50% of our pricing in the Al Tasnim opp.
competitors.how_we_win: Speed-to-value via pre-built SAP B1/S4HANA/Yardi connectors vs GEP's custom build; unified three-ERP experience; regional Middle East team; SaaS-fit contracting. Protect the win by keeping Phase 1 green so GEP has no re-entry.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Mair already tried and abandoned Coupa as overcomplicated; Zycus positioned as simpler and fit for the three-ERP environment.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ariba licences acquired but written off.
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba is unused inventory; Mair moved on before Zycus engaged.

competitors.how_we_win: Buy-over-build already chosen; Zycus pre-built connectors beat custom integration effort.

items.grounding_quote: Hrishi is going to rewrite the the payment schedule ... splitting out the design sign off into two different phases

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 2 (Negative). This is a traditional procurement-automation sale: scope is iContract/iSource/eProcurement with no Merlin or Agentic AI, and across 6 months of negotiation the buyer never raised AI. AI is not a near-term lever here; the winning value story was integration speed, phased delivery and SaaS-fit contracting, not AI.

items.quote: our legal team have said their preference is to use our paper ... it's fit for purpose ... Saas kind of fit for purpose
we can split that phase one, phase two, phase 3 design ... we can further split it
items.criterion: Phased rollout and flexible payment milestones

items.quote: if we need to get things done from Yadi, there's a dependency. If they take longer time, my project is at risk
items.criterion: Pre-built integration to SAP S/4HANA, SAP B1 and Yardi

items.quote: the subscription should commence once the product has been fully developed and actively put into use
items.criterion: Billing aligned to product go-live, not upfront

items.quote: we've already reduced the implementation cost by over 25% ... it's a significant amount

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong overall fit that carried Zycus to signature; the live exposure now is the timing of the licence invoice versus go-live, plus the flagged Yardi third-party integration dependency.

hard.ais_why:
• Across 6,960 chars of dated rep notes spanning Nov 2024 to May 2026 ‚Äî covering disco call, demos, RFP submission, BAFO, MSA negotiation and LOI ‚Äî there is zero buyer-side or rep-side AI mention. Product scope is iContract; iSource; eProcurement (no Merlin, no Agentic AI, no ANA, no Merlin Intake). The 7 May 2026 entry quotes Head of Proc Ops: 'Everything appears to be ok for approval today/tomorrow & LOI early next week' ‚Äî pure commercial close, not AI.
• On 21 Apr 2026: 'F2F meeting went well, asking us to sweeten the deal in year 1, but in return have agreed to 5 years.' On 24 Apr 2026: 'They acknowledged receipt of BAFO, are reviewing will revert next week.' Discussions are about pricing, travel budget split, MSA paper, RTO/RPO concerns ‚Äî competitive with GEP at 50% of Zycus pricing. AI features never surface as differentiator or buyer ask.
• Confidence is High: 6 months of late-stage negotiation detail with no AI signal whatsoever and no AI SKU in scope. This is a traditional procurement automation deal evaluated on price, implementation phasing, and SaaS-vs-on-premise considerations.
hard.next_step:
Confidence 100%

20th June - Year 1 licences invoice issued.

18th June - Sales order completed, PO shared with finance.

17th June - Full initialled/stamped/signed copy received.

12th June - 11th hour issues resolved, contract updated, signed by Carl and shared with CPO, he has confirmed he will be signing it today. SIGNED SIGNATURE PAGE RECEIVED.

11th June - CPO is trying to push through some additional negotiations at the 11th hour, most are OK, working with Carl to formulate a response.

10th June - The Legal-2-legal call happed today with the CPO Mazin in attendance, There was one issue they (Mazin + Legal) were VERY firm on regarding arbitration (They insisted on AD courts, which creates a language issue ... compared to a Dubai option which is in English) - after some strong push back Zoheb conceded (it is a Mair Group board resolution) to their demand, so all issues now resolved. Final sanity check undertaken by both sides, final clean copy shared and signed by Zycus (Carl) and awaiting their counter signature tomorrow.

9th June - Final markups received today, Zoheb reviewed quickly and reverted - some back & forth and a hard push from them on termination for convenience, with Zoheb & Carl's help we submitted and they accepted the standard clause, all outstanding issues appear resolved and a clean final version has been shared, but they want a call on Weds 10th afternoon to discuss, if all well on that call we should be able to push for immediate signature.

4th June - Some meetings & positive activity today, most of the legal responses are ready to share with us, just 2 IT elements they need to resolve internally, then they will share the feedback (won't say what they are at this stage, but say nothing to worry about) - pushing for today but they won't commit, so likely tomorrow (Fri)..... it's clear now their legal are actively pushing back on a call ... maybe after this feedback we can try again if needed. Mazin (CPO) is back today (he was ill for a few days), I have asked him for his help progressing this - he is not promising but I know he is asking questions in the background which will help, he is sticking to his point that it's the use of our paper that's causing delays (not really true, they have had the templates for quite some time, and we were open to using theirs if quicker).... I am still pushing for a sig tomorrow, but realistically looking unlikely as there will be another markup iteration to work through.

3rd June - Proc team are still pushing legal and are now expecting an update tomorrow (Thurs) - I keep pushing the legal2Legal call but they are not biting.. They tell me there are no issues, lets see when we get the feedback no one has mentioned T4C, and I am not reminding them. Mazin the CPO is off again today, so no response on the signing 'ceremony' (they think he is back tomorrow), I am still pushing for ASAP/Friday.

2nd June - Proc team are chasing legal, hoping to have it resolved Thurs latest, they are not aware of any issues/showstoppers - I reiterated the call option with Zoheb and they will push their own legal again to arrange (if needed).... The CPO/signatory is off today so no answer on the signing ceremony with Carl, I am pushing for Thurs or Fri, they understand the urgency of booking your travel and will update me tomorrow on his return- if they agree to sig Friday it's a good sign for the contract negotiations/T4C.

1st June - No update last week as the team were all off for the Eid public holiday, caught up this morning for an update: they have a meeting with Legal today to get an update on progress, they have agreed to a direct call with Zoheb and their legal (Omar) early this week to resolve any outstanding issues and move towards signature.

25th May - MSA & OF1 review underway, initial MG/Zycus markups shared on MSA, no counters/follow-ups from Mair yet, chased Mon 25th - pushing to get it resolved asap, now likely after Eid public holiday (for rest of the week).

22nd May - MSA and Order 1 close to completion with Mair legal- will have full update by Monday 25th 

May 19th - Redlines on MSA received today, (thanks to Zoheb for a quick turnaround) - some back and forth on clauses and a negotiation needed on a termination for convenience - reverted.

May 18th - Following emergency below, Mair head of legal has agreed to continue reviewing our paper, feedback/redlines expected Tues 19th or Weds 20th (Zoheb continues to review theirs in parallel).

May 14th - Head of legal working on our paper has had to leave the country for a family emergency, unclear on his availability to resolve currently - Zoheb is working on their template as a fallback and will update me on 19th May.

May 13th - Lovemer has a meeting on 14th with Omar (Mair Head of Legal) to discuss the MSA/OF will update us asap afterwards with feedback.

May 12th - Reminded them for the SOW workshop - Hrishi +1 (& Dan) will travel and hold onsite to minimise any risk, date TBC.

May 11th - Mair legal are reviewing Zycus MSA and hopeful to agree on using our paper- will know in next 2 days (this will greatly accelerate signing)

No immediate blockers in Ts & Cs.

May 8th - LOI received - likely MSA/Order 1 sign off "before Eid break" (i.e. before 26th May) - pushing for sooner.

May 7th - All issues resolved, some back & forth on implementation phasing, but nothing to worry about, verbal comment from Head of Proc Ops: "Everything appears to be ok for approval today/tomorrow & LOI early next week".

May 6th - Meeting below held: (positive session), mostly based on clarifications of: a) implementation invoicing across phases (resolved), b) number of onsite teams (resolved), c) travel budget (Outstanding - they are asking us to swallow it all - we are pushing back, to split 50/50 as this stage - action for them to confirm, otherwise potentially intro Carl to Mazin-CPO), d) we also raised the legal paperwork, their policy/preference is their own standard template, (Outstanding - not a showstopper, but made a case that our paper is Saas/Zycus fit for purpose and faster/easier to edit than rewriting theirs, action for them to confirm). Intention is to resolve these issues by the end of the week, ideally then to submit LOI by EOW (Fri 8th May) and signed Contracts before Eid public holiday (w/c Mon 25th May).

May 4th - Provided some clarifications (Implementation onsite support, payment terms, travel) - they followed up with a request for a call on Weds 6th to discuss: Phased Implementation plan timeline, Payment Terms and Travel fees - Carl, Tanya & Hrishi are joining, (also meeting on 5th to strategize).

30th April/May 1st - Some back & forth clarifications, nothing concerning.

29th April - Comment from CPO after a nudge: "Still going through some internal review process. Hope to close this asap".

27th April - Requested (already sent) and resent MSA & Oder Form templates for internal review, also completed a vendor registration form and provided documentation (Trade Licence, TRN, Bank Details etc.), made clear that using our paper is preferred but not a showstopper.

24th April - They acknowledged receipt of BAFO, are reviewing will revert next week, looking like they will push for their paper, asked to discuss with CPO as ours is Saas/Zycus fit for purpose, faster, mutual, no risk etc. etc.

23rd April - Liaised with Carl offered some BAFO concessions (through discount options held over for this phase e.g. Publicity 5%), license pricing mostly protected in return for: a) min 5 years, b) strong reference-ability/PR and c) speed of decision (end May signature latest) - BAFO & MSA/Oder Form templates shared - Next Step: requested a call with CPO Fri/Sat/Sun to discuss.

21st April - F2F meeting went well, asking us to sweeten the deal in year 1, but in return have agreed to 5 years (started the meeting committing to only 3), will be a reference customer and a quick decision - followed up with an mail confirming & asking for closing BAFO price with preference to Zycus- some niggly terms we should be able to navigate, due Thursday, with formal decision expected by mid-May, liaising with Carl to review.

16th April - Next step is a face-to-face meeting in Abu Dhabi to discuss commercials & implementation, Dan & Tanya will be on-site with Hrishi dialling in - will synch with Carl prior to discuss commercial tactics.

9th April - Clarifications and revised implementation pricing (now a longer phased approach) hand delivered - retained original SaaS pricing in expectation of a negotiation (champion tip), made clear the assumption is a single phased contract, digital copies also added to their Showpad link. NOTE: Some issues where we were not able to meet their requirements/match GEP i.e. RTO/RPO (concerns due to current war situation and AWS taken out in Dubai & Bahrain) - champion thinks it won't be a showstopper.

6th April - Revised phasing and further formal clarifications received - response to be hard copy/hand delivered to Abu Dhabi office Thurs latest.

3rd April - Positive clarifications call, some push to improve RTO/RPO and uptime to match the competition, but doesn't appear to be a showstopper, also looking to move away from big bang to a phased approach focussing on priority modules e.g. Sourcing first, not clear economic impact yet, but have indicated it will still be a single contract - follow up call with the champion afterwards confirmed informally we are the preferred vendor and are in competition with GEP, next steps they are going to confirm on 6th/7th what their phasing plan will be and we will need to review/update commercials accordingly - note GEP are 50% of our pricing in the Al Tasnim opp.

1st April - Clarification responses run-through call scheduled for Fri 3rd April.

30th Mar - Clarifications received and responses submitted.

25th March - Nudged Lovemer for an update on evaluation & timelines, Vishal (CTO) called later to clarify some RFP response elements, potentially a good sign as he said he is not supposed to do it and it's against the proc policies to give us an advantage, will clarify and respond asap today/tomorrow.

6th March - RFP Technical & Commercial response hand delivered to HQ in Abu Dhabi, response clarifications is next step. (Commercial positioned as a budgetary estimate as we had to guesstimate many data points, also held back on some discounts in anticipation of further negotiation).

26th Feb - Clarifications received (still many gaps, will need to guesstimate and make caveats in pricing) - deadline extended to 6th March - still expecting a hand delivered hard copy to Abu Dhabi.

10th Feb - RFP for S2P received - deadline is 25th Feb, clarifications 18th.... currently requires hard copy submission to Abu Dhabi, requested Showpad or alternative.
